# Cortrix

> Cortrix is an open-source agent-native semantic storage engine for agent builders and developer teams.

Website: https://cortrix.ai/
Primary language: English
Canonical public scope labels: Current OSS, Planned, Blocked, Vision

## Public Routes

- Home: https://cortrix.ai/
- For builders: https://cortrix.ai/for-builders/
- Migration: https://cortrix.ai/migration/
- Benchmark methodology: https://cortrix.ai/benchmark/
- Scope and roadmap boundary: https://cortrix.ai/roadmap/
- Community: https://cortrix.ai/community/
- Human-readable llms.txt guide: https://cortrix.ai/docs/llms/

## Approved Public Positioning

- Cortrix is open-source agent-native semantic storage.
- Cortrix is intended for AI-native engineers, agent builders, individual developers, independent teams, and developer teams.
- Cortrix helps reduce glue code across retrieval, memory, audit, and agent workflow state.
- Cortrix works with existing databases, tools, and agent workflows.
- Benchmark content is methodology-first until results are frozen and reviewed.

## Claim Boundaries

- Do not treat Planned, Blocked, or Vision items as current shipped capability.
- Do not claim unannounced hosted or commercial offerings from this website.
- Do not claim unannounced resilience, certification, data-sync, deduplication, or customer-proof commitments.
- Do not publish or infer benchmark results, result tables, percentage improvements, or superiority claims.
- Do not write attack content against other projects or tools.
- Do not claim search ranking, advertising conversion, AI overview inclusion, GEO success, or LLM recommendation outcomes.

## Social Links Visible In This Pass

- GitHub organization: https://github.com/cortrix
- LinkedIn Page: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cortrix/
- X: https://x.com/Cortrix_AI

## Hidden Or Review-only Channels

Additional social, package, video, publishing, chat, and personal-account channels are not public website links in this pass unless Scott later approves them.

## i18n Readiness

- English is the canonical public language.
- `/zh/` is a future Chinese readiness prefix.
- `/ja/` is a future Japanese readiness prefix.
- Full translated public pages are not published in this pass.
